title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
Errors in JavaScript can be caught and handled gracefully using try/catch/finally.

Built-in Error types:
• Error — generic base.
• TypeError — wrong type (e.g., calling null as a function).
• ReferenceError — accessing an undeclared variable.
• SyntaxError — invalid JS syntax (thrown at parse time).
• RangeError — value out of allowed range.

Best practices:
• Catch only what you can handle; re-throw what you can't.
• Use finally for cleanup (close files, hide loading spinners).
• Create custom Error subclasses for domain-specific errors.
• In async code, combine try/catch with await.
• Don't swallow errors silently with an empty catch block.
